sanskrit:
त्यक्त्वा कर्मफलासङ्गं नित्यतृप्तो निराश्रयः।
कर्मण्यभिप्रवृत्तोऽपि नैव किञ्चित्करोति सः॥
transliteration:
tyaktvā karma-phalāsaṅgaṃ nitya-tṛpto nirāśrayaḥ |
karmaṇy abhipravṛtto ’pi naiva kiñcit karoti saḥ ||
literal_meaning: Having abandoned attachment to the fruits of action, ever-content, without any support, though fully engaged in activity, he indeed does nothing at all.
contextual_meaning: A person who has internalized that self-worth is not contingent on outcomes experiences 'flow'—intense engagement without existential anxiety.
psychological_state: Secure-functioning: action is intrinsically rewarding; success/failure do not threaten identity.
trigger_scenario: A coder writes open-source software for the joy of solving problems; downloads, bugs, and praise are watched with interest but do not alter her self-esteem or sleep.
anchor_text: Work wholeheartedly, untouched by craving for results.
